page: Amazon Web Services c9gd.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this server features 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz with a dedicated CPU allocation. Because there is only one thread per core, workloads benefit from consistent execution performance without resource sharing at the core level.

The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, the c9gd.16xlarge includes 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, providing high-speed, low-latency local data access. This hardware configuration is managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor, which minimizes virtualization overhead.

Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 34 Gbps. Given the combination of dedicated arm64 compute cores, a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and substantial local NVMe storage, this instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads that also require high-speed local scratch space. Suitable workloads include distributed analytics, batch processing, ad serving, and high-performance web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c9gd.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by 64-core AWS Graviton arm64 processors running at 2.8 GHz. Utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides 64 dedicated vCPUs with a single thread per core, ensuring consistent performance for compute-heavy tasks. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. Storage needs are met by 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, which deliver high-speed, low-latency data access. Additionally, the server supports a baseline network bandwidth of 34 Gbps. This hardware profile makes the c9gd.16xlarge highly efficient for workloads requiring a balance of dedicated arm64 compute power and fast local storage, such as distributed databases, batch processing, and web serving.
